methodology.harness: Basquin valve + agent on Tomcat 9, soft invariants (latency>25ms, heapDelta>256KB)
methodology.driver: HttpRouteDriveTarget, single-flight, ~20 req/s, 12000 requests per app
methodology.note: All numbers from real runs. Server-side findings from the in-JVM valve; client latency from k6 at 1 VU (matches the single-flight driver, avoids the valve's serialization-under-concurrency). No fabricated figures.
methodology.coverage_guided_gap: This pass ran the round-robin/load driver; JaCoCo coverage-guided-vs-naive is a documented future addition (needs JaCoCo in each app JVM).

k6_10vu_serialized_ms.note: valve serializes (DD-005) so concurrent latency queues

headline.claim: A crash-only oracle (a plain fuzzer, a load tool's error count) reports 0 of these 16,701 availability findings — both apps served every request with a 2xx/3xx while allocating up to 10.7MB (JSPWiki) / 15.6MB (JPetStore) on a single unmodified request.
